title: Isolation of live single cells from intestinal biopsy
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"5e6h4","text":"This protocol describes dissociation of a human intestinal biopsy tissue into single cells, followed by depletion of dead cells via annexin V MACS beads. The outcome is a single-cell suspension with viability ≥ 90% that is used for single-cell sequencing or establishemt of enteroid/colonoid culture.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Prepare reagents: 5 mL Cryo-wash (4.5mL PBS + 500μL FBS) 1.5 mL Enzyme Mix (30μL Liberase TH stock + 30μL DNAse I stock + 3mL HBSS) 10 mL Blocker Buffer (4% BSA in PBS: reconstitute 400mg BSA in 10mL PBS, filter-sterilize) 2 mL Cell Reconstitution Buffer (2mL Advanced DMEM:F12 + 50μL Blocker Buffer + 20μL DNAse I) 20 mL 1x MACS Binding Buffer (1mL 20x concentrate + 19mL sterile ddH2O* ) * Do not substitute with deionized water (this will impair annexin V binding)
Thaw the tissue quickly in a 37 °C water bath If using a bead bath, put a beaker with pre-heated water into the beads and place the cryovial in the water

As soon as the vial contents are liquified, transfer the tissue suspension to an eppendorf tube We finely mince the biopsy tissue before cryopreservation. If you are working with a biopsy that was cryopreserved whole, do not skip step 6
Spin the tube 30s - 700g , discard the supernatant 700 x g, °C, 30 seconds Room temperature
Wash the tissue (repeat 3 times)
Resuspend the pellet in 1 mL Cryo-wash
Spin the tube 30s - 700g, discard the supernatant 700 x g, °C, 30 seconds Room temperature
Skip this step if biopsy was minced before cryopreservation
Reconstitute the washed tissue in 500 µL Cryo-wash supplemented with 5 µL DNAse I
Mince the tissue inside the eppendorf tube using sterile surgical scissors
Spin the tube 30s - 700g, discard the supernatant 700 x g, °C, 30 seconds Room temperature
Resuspend the pellet in 1 mL Enzyme Mix
Incubate in a thermomixer 37 o C - 15m, 800RPM 800 rpm, 37 °C,
During digestion, place a 100μm strainer in a 50mL conical and pre-wet the membrane with with 5 mL Blocker Buffer
After digestion, let the tissue fragments settle by gravity to the bottom, pass the supernatant through prepared strainer from step 8.1
Repeat the digestion (steps 7-9) with fresh Enzyme Mix 2 more times (3 cycles of 15m total)
After the final digest, pass the tissue fragments 10 times through a P1000 tip
Pass the digested suspension through the strainer:
Apply the suspension from step 11 directly to the strainer membrane
Take the strainer out of the conical tube and place it in a round 30mm TC dish A larger size dish can be used in lieu of a 30mm dish
Use the flat surface of an insulin syringe plunger to force the remaining undigested fragments through the strainer by rubbing the plunger in a circular motion, discard the plunger
Rinse the strainer by lifting it slightly from the round dish and applying 2 mL Blocker Buffer, capturing the flow-through in the 30mm dish; keep the strainer suspended
Rinse the strainer bottom with 2 mL Blocker Buffer, capturing it in the 30mm dish
Transfer the suspension from the 30mm dish to the 50mL conical from step 8.1
Rinse the 30mm dish with 1 mL Blocker Buffer and transfer to the same conical
Transfer the suspension from 50mL conical to a new 15mL conical. This step is optional, but recommended, because it results in a more visible pellet than when spinning in a 50mL conical
Spin down the cells 5m - 500g, resuspend in 1 mL Cell Reconstitution Buffer 500 x g, °C, 5 minutes Room temperature
Strain the suspension into a FACS tube through the 35μm strainer cap It is essential to strain the cells before proceeding to column-based dead cell removal
Count the cells, noting the viability
Pellet the cells: 5m - 500g, discard the supernatant 500 x g, °C, 5 minutes Room temperature
Resuspend the pellet in 200 µL of MACS Dead Cell Removal MicroBeads
Incubate 15m - room temperature 00:15:00 Room temperature
During the incubation, prepare the MACS LS column by placing it into the magnet and rinsing with 3 mL MACS Binding Buffer (discard the flow-through)
Add 300 µL MACS Binding Buffer to the suspension from step 19 to total volume of 500 µL

Apply the suspension to the prepared MACS LS column (in the magnet) and collect the flow-through into a 15mL conical tube The flow-through contains live cells, while dead cells are captured in the column

Wash the column 4 times with 3 mL MACS Binding Buffer, keep collecting the flow-through (live cells) into the same tube. At this point the column can be discarded, unless you are troubleshooting and intend to elute the captured dead cells.
Spin the live cell suspension 5m - 500g, discard the supernatant 500 x g, °C, 5 minutes Room temperature
Resuspend the pellet in 500 µL Cell Reconstitution Buffer
Count the cells, noting the viability
creator: Tatiana Karakasheva
